{{Quote|Merr-Sonn was catering specifically to pirates when they came up with this baby. (Though it is also suited for law-enforcement use, as well; boarding parties beware…CorSec has started using 'em.)|[[Meydhu]]|<ref name="Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref>}}
The '''Deck Sweeper Stunning Blaster''' was a [[Stun/Legends|stun]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="PP" /> Based on [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech Industries]]' [[Firespray DL-87 stun rifle]],<ref name="AEG" /> the Deck Sweeper only fired on the stun setting and had no lethal option.<ref name="SV">[[Scum and Villainy (sourcebook)|''Scum and Villainy'']]</ref> The Deck Sweeper was designed to stun a large number of targets at once; the wide barrel at the front of the weapon fired in a forty-five degree arc with a range up to ten [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="PP" />

The Deck Sweeper was sold exclusively to [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agencies]] and military forces for use by customs boarding parties and riot control police. Civilian possession of the weapon was illegal, but it was favoured by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]] as a boarding weapon.The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rejected the Deck Sweeper for [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] use, citing the weapon's short range and slow rate of fire. The bulky size of the weapon was also a factor, with Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] believing they were impractical for [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[marine]]s.<ref name="PP" />
